questions.text: Which of these is a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Influenza (flu) is caused by a virus. Typhoid and cholera are bacterial, malaria is protozoan.
Vaccination introduces weakened or dead pathogens to stimulate immunity, helping prevent future infections.

questions.text: What is the difference between acute and chronic diseases?
questions.correctAnswer: Acute diseases last for short period, chronic diseases last for long period or lifetime.
questions.explanation: Acute: sudden onset, short duration (e.g., common cold). Chronic: long-lasting (e.g., diabetes, cancer).

questions.text: The vector for malaria is ________ mosquito.
questions.explanation: Female Anopheles mosquito transmits the malaria parasite (Plasmodium) from infected to healthy persons.
